subject: [PATCH v1] fuse: fix io-uring background queue stall on request completion

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary:
The reviewer, Bernd Schubert, disagrees that the patch fixes a real issue and suggests an alternative solution to prevent background requests from getting stuck in the io-uring per-queue bg queue. He points out that fuse_uring_flush_bg() already allows at least one background request per queue to pass through when fc->active_background is below the limit, and proposes to always let through at least one request per queue.

The reviewer noted that the patch fixes an issue where only one request can run at a time, but did not provide further technical details or suggestions for improvement.

The reviewer noted that there might be a stuck background queue issue at teardown, and mentioned that Horst is investigating this separately.
reviews.raw_body:
I don't think it ever gets stuck. In fuse_uring_flush_bg()

        while ((fc->active_background < fc->max_background ||
                !queue->active_background) &&


And queue->active_background gets decreased in the caller,
fuse_uring_req_end. Idea is to always let through at least one 
background request per queue. Reson is that the global 
fc->num_background might be at the limit already, a 
queue then might get a request, it would get added to 
queue->fuse_req_bg_queue, but once fc->active_background goes 
down, there wouldn't be anything to wake up these requests. 
Issue: Only one request allowed per queue when this comes up.

---

This basically solves the issue that situations could come up where only
one requests runs at a time.

---

Reviewed-by: Bernd Schubert <bernd@bsbernd.com>



PS: If you should be chasing a stuck bg queue issue at tear down, Horst
is chasing a teardown issue with bg queue. 
We are currently testing this patch

diff --git a/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c b/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c
index 3de97ed2280f..451bf8981e19 100644
--- a/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c
+++ b/fs/fuse/dev_uring.c
@@ -136,11 +136,10 @@ void fuse_uring_abort_end_requests(struct fuse_ring *ring)
                if (!queue)
                        continue;

-               queue->stopped = true;
-
                WARN_ON_ONCE(ring->fc->max_background != UINT_MAX);
                spin_lock(&queue->lock);
                spin_lock(&fc->bg_lock);
+               queue->stopped = true;
                fuse_uring_flush_bg(queue);
                spin_unlock(&fc->bg_lock);
                spin_unlock(&queue->lock);


Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the io-uring background queue flush needs to be reordered to account for connection-level background counters before flushing the queue's background queue, and agreed to restructure in a future version.

Author acknowledged that the patch's wording might be unclear regarding the condition under which pending background requests are stuck, and asked for a rewording suggestion.

Author acknowledges that the issue is being investigated by another developer and expresses appreciation for their work.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Bernd,

Thanks for taking a look at this.

---

If the queue already has other background requests in-flight, then
this check never passes due to the stale fc->active_background value
and all pending background requests on the queue are stuck until that
in-flight background request completes, no? I'm rereading my commit
message, maybe the wording is unclear - do you prefer it to be
reworded to "which can leave pending background requests in the
per-queue background queue stalled"?

---

Awesome, looking forward to seeing Horst's investigation.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer Bernd Schubert agrees with the patch's solution but disagrees with the term 'stall' used in its description, suggesting a more accurate word choice.
reviews.raw_body:
certainly agree with the fix, just not on the word "stalled".


Cheers,
Bernd

2026-04-01.patch_summary: This patch fixes a stall in the io-uring background queue when a request completes. The issue arises from the fact that the connection-level background counters are not properly accounted for before flushing the queue's background queue, leaving pending requests stuck. To fix this, the patch accounts for the connection-level counters first and then flushes the queue's background queue, ensuring that pending requests are dispatched correctly.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author agreed to restructure the patch in version 2 based on reviewer's suggested wording, indicating a fix is planned.
reviews.raw_body:
Sounds good,m I'll send out a v2 that uses your wording.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch fixes a stall in the io-uring background queue when a request completes. The issue arises from the fact that the connection-level background counters are not properly accounted for before flushing the queue's background queue, leaving pending requests stuck. To fix this, the patch accounts for the connection-level counters first and then flushes the queue's background queue, ensuring that pending requests are dispatched correctly.
